words.clue: Transparent anterior 1/6 of the fibrous coat responsible for 2/3 of refractive power
Junction between the cornea and sclera containing limbal stem cells
Highly vascular middle coat layer (uvea) that nourishes the outer retina
Circular muscle of the iris that constricts the pupil (miosis)
Central depression of the macula with highest visual acuity and only cones
Sieve-like area where the optic nerve pierces the sclera posteriorly
Condition caused by obstruction of aqueous humour drainage raising intraocular pressure
Opacity of the lens causing gradual painless loss of vision, commonest cause of blindness in India
Fibres connecting the ciliary body to the lens (suspensory ligament)
Photoreceptors responsible for colour and photopic vision, concentrated at the fovea
